top_entities.name: Walt Disney
top_entities.news_summary: 1,000 employees laid off April 14 including entire home entertainment/EPK teams, 8% of Marvel, marketing/publicity at Hulu/FX/ESPN/ABC News. CEO cited AI-driven efficiency rationale. $95-130M restructuring charge. Entire teams eliminated with 4-month severance.

top_entities.name: Amazon
top_entities.news_summary: Worker died at Troutdale Oregon PDX9 fulfillment center April 6 — management ordered employees to work around body for 1+ hour post-death. NLRB April 3 ordered Amazon to recognize and bargain with Teamsters at Staten Island JFK8 — first union to win bargaining order against Amazon. Amazon appealing.

top_entities.name: Pfizer
top_entities.news_summary: Pfizer raised prices on 80 products in 2026 — most of any company. Comirnaty up 15%, Ibrance, Paxlovid also raised. Despite Trump pricing deal. Senate Democrat report: 350+ medicines raised across 16 deal-signatories. Washington state drug pricing law lawsuit March 2026.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.news_summary: Q1 2026 earnings April 21 amid DOJ criminal Medicare Advantage fraud investigation, securities class-action (misleading financial statements), Catholic nuns shareholder suit, 401k misuse class-actions. DOJ structural antitrust challenge ongoing. Stock down 54% from high. Earnings call is imminent watch item.

top_entities.name: China
top_entities.news_summary: UN experts January 2026: Uyghur/Tibetan/Kazakh forced labor may amount to crimes against humanity. HRW April 1 urged Canada to speak out on forced labor. Coercive state labor transfer programs documented. Xinjiang supplies 20% global cotton, 45% solar polysilicon through tainted supply chains.

top_entities.name: Saudi Arabia
top_entities.news_summary: HRW January 2026: 356 executions in 2025 — second consecutive annual record. 183 foreign nationals executed mostly for nonlethal drug offenses. Death penalty used to suppress peaceful dissent. 70-year-old academic died in prison April. Torture-tainted confessions systemic.

top_entities.name: GEO Group
top_entities.news_summary: Hugo Gonzalez v. GEO Group trial underway in April 2026. Public Justice motion to intervene granted April 13 to unseal 50+ court documents and video footage of June 2020 use-of-force at Adelanto ICE facility. ICE detainee death surge documented April 19. Chemical exposure class-action also active.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.news_summary: Global Sumud Flotilla of 39 boats departed Spain April 16 heading to Gaza. 1,000 activists carrying aid to break blockade. Amnesty called on states to protect flotilla April 20. Israel continues blockade; 37 NGOs banned; only 19/37 hospitals partly functional; only Kerem Shalom crossing open.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: UN Fact-Finding Mission March 2026: repression may amount to crimes against humanity. 13 political executions since March 19. Internet shutdown persists. Torture death of Abbas Yavari documented. Infrastructure attacks impacting civilian food/health/housing. NCRI daily briefs April 19-20 confirm ongoing repression.

top_entities.name: Russia
top_entities.news_summary: April 16 attack: nearly 700 drones + ballistic missiles targeting civilian areas, 16 killed, 80+ injured. April 4 Kryvyi Rih attack killed 9 children on playground. UN/Atlantic Council report: systematic civilian targeting to depopulate Ukraine. Ukraine FM: war crimes that must be stopped.

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: War entered fourth year April 15; 33.7M require assistance; famine confirmed in El Fasher and Kadugli. WFP food fell 14% due to funding crisis (only 16.2% funded). Berlin conference raised 1B+ euros but crisis called abandoned. 800,000 children under 5 severely malnourished.

top_entities.name: South Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: UNMISS mandate expires April 30 — Security Council vote imminent. HRW April 13: Do not diminish peacekeeping mission. Mission reduced by one-third due to US funding cuts; 40% fewer patrols; 40+ human rights monitoring missions canceled. Active SSPDF/SPLA-IO conflict. Security Council briefing April 17.

top_entities.name: Haiti
top_entities.news_summary: Gangs control 90% of Port-au-Prince. New multinational security force troops arriving April 2026 creating potential crossfire risk for civilians. 6.4M in need including 2.8M children. 5.7M facing acute hunger. UN seeks $880M, received only 27% in 2025. 30 killed in Gran Grif attack March 28-29.

top_entities.name: xAI/Grok
top_entities.news_summary: NAACP filed lawsuit April 14 against xAI: 27 illegal unpermitted gas turbines in Southaven MS at Colossus 2 data center. 1,700 tons NOx potential annual emissions in area already failing smog standards. Formaldehyde and fine particulate matter also released. Disproportionate harm to majority-Black Boxtown neighborhood. Earthjustice/SELC representing plaintiffs.

top_entities.name: Venezuela
top_entities.news_summary: Amnesty International April 2026: amnesty law being used as mechanism of repression. 485+ remain arbitrarily detained as of April 6. EU condemned repression March 2026. 600,000 Venezuelans face US deportation. Selective implementation of amnesty law documented by multiple human rights organizations.

sector_alerts.alert: Illinois SB 3261 committee hearing scheduled April 24 creates direct regulatory risk for AI labs. OpenAI and Anthropic have taken opposing sides — OpenAI backing a liability shield bill, Anthropic supporting mandatory safety plans and child harm liability. First state to bifurcate major labs on liability.

US Supreme Court April 17 ruled 8-0 that Chevron and ExxonMobil can move Louisiana environmental lawsuits to federal court, jeopardizing $745M state jury verdict and affecting 11 of 42 similar oil company lawsuits. Backed by Trump administration. Major structural shift in state climate litigation strategy.

Five simultaneous tier-1 humanitarian emergencies: Sudan war year four (33.7M in need), South Sudan UNMISS mandate expiry April 30, Iran ongoing post-war repression (UN: crimes against humanity), Israel-Gaza flotilla confrontation approaching, DRC-M23 humanitarian access negotiations. UN emergency mechanisms all active.

GEO Group Adelanto trial active; court unsealed documents April 13. CoreCivic expanded capacity in Kansas March 2026. ICE detention reached all-time high of 66,000. Death surge in ICE facilities documented April 19. H.R.1 budget bill enables further private detention expansion.

Amazon faced dual major labor events in April: worker death at Troutdale OR facility April 6 (management ordered staff to work around body) and NLRB April 3 bargaining order (first ever union win against Amazon). Both events are material compassion-relevant evidence requiring reassessment.

NAACP/Earthjustice/SELC filed lawsuit April 14 against xAI (Elon Musk) for operating 27 illegal unpermitted gas turbines near Memphis data center. 1,700 tons NOx potential annual emissions in majority-Black neighborhood already failing smog standards. Environmental justice case sets precedent for AI infrastructure accountability.

April 16 Russian attack: nearly 700 drones + ballistic missiles targeting residential areas, 16 civilians killed, 80+ injured. April 4 Kryvyi Rih attack killed 9 children on playground. UN report confirms systematic civilian targeting. Ongoing pattern of war crimes in Ukraine.

Snap (16% workforce, 1,000 jobs, April 15-16) and Disney (1,000 jobs, April 14) both cited AI-driven efficiency as rationale for major layoffs in same week. Represents emerging pattern of AI displacement impacting workforce welfare across Fortune 500 in April 2026.

entity_reviews.name: xAI/Grok
entity_reviews.summary: NAACP filed lawsuit April 14 against xAI: 27 illegal unpermitted gas turbines in Southaven MS at Colossus 2 data center. 1,700 tons NOx potential annual emissions in area already failing smog standards. Formaldehyde and fine particulate matter also released. Disproportionate harm to majority-Black Boxtown neighborhood. Earthjustice/SELC representing plaintiffs.

entity_reviews.name: DeepSeek
entity_reviews.summary: March 2026: German BSI confirmed DeepSeek inadvertently transmitted classified parliamentary metadata to Shanghai servers. Multiple government bans (Italy, NY, Texas, Virginia). No country audit found compliant with domestic AI/privacy law. Keystroke/IP/file data stored on China servers.
